articles.title: Prediction Market Risk Is Hiding in Your Organization Whether You Know It or Not
articles.summary: Every company makes dozens of implicit predictions daily about customer behavior, market conditions, and operational outcomes, but most executives don't recognize these forecasts as bets that could blow up their strategy. Smart organizations are starting to formalize these hidden predictions into trackable markets, giving them better odds of spotting disasters before they hit the bottom line.

articles.title: Kalshi and Polymarket vie for Washington influence as Congress contemplates regulation
articles.summary: Kalshi and Polymarket are racing to build political connections on Capitol Hill as lawmakers consider new rules for prediction markets. Whoever wins the lobbying game could shape regulations that determine which platforms thrive and what Americans can actually bet on.

articles.title: Prediction market boom forecast spurs gains in Robinhood and Coinbase, Bernstein says $1T potential
articles.summary: Bernstein analysts say prediction markets could hit $1T in potential value, driving stock gains for Robinhood and Coinbase as the platforms position themselves in the space. Wall Street finally gets it: prediction markets aren't just political betting curiosities, they're the next frontier for retail trading platforms looking to capture billions in user engagement and fees.

articles.title: Prediction markets will grow to $1 trillion by 2030, Bernstein estimates
articles.summary: Bernstein analysts predict the prediction markets industry will explode from its current size to $1 trillion by 2030. If they're right, we're looking at the mainstreaming of prediction markets from niche curiosity to legitimate financial infrastructure that could reshape how we price everything from elections to earnings.

articles.title: High Roller stock soars as much as 130% on Crypto.com prediction market agreement
articles.summary: High Roller's stock rocketed 130% after announcing a partnership deal with Crypto.com's prediction markets platform. The surge signals that traditional gambling stocks are betting big on prediction markets as the next growth engine, especially with crypto-native platforms leading the charge.

articles.title: Prediction Markets Have an Insider Trading Problem. Are They Still Worth the Gamble?
articles.summary: Prediction markets are grappling with traders who bet on outcomes they can directly influence or have privileged information about, undermining the whole point of crowd-sourced forecasting. If insiders keep gaming the system, these markets lose their credibility as information aggregators and become just another rigged casino that smart money avoids.

articles.title: Introducing The Prediction Market Startups Database
articles.summary: We've compiled a comprehensive database tracking every startup building in the prediction markets space, from established players like Kalshi to scrappy newcomers barely out of stealth mode. This gives bettors and investors their first clear view of who's actually building the future of predictive betting and where the smart money is flowing.

articles.title: ‘Cowboy’: Michael Selig, a top Trump Wall Street regulator, comes out swinging
articles.summary: Michael Selig, a key Trump-era Wall Street regulator, is making aggressive moves that signal a major shift in financial oversight policy. His confrontational approach could reshape market regulations and create new volatility in financial sector stocks, making regulatory policy bets suddenly very interesting.

articles.title: Kalshi Is Half Right About Prediction Markets and Gambling
articles.summary: Kalshi's latest argument defending prediction markets as non-gambling tools gets the economics right but misses how regulators actually think about risk-based entertainment. The distinction matters because half-baked defenses could backfire when the next regulatory crackdown hits and operators need bulletproof arguments about social utility.

articles.title: Is Polymarket a threat to democracy? The money is simply too good
articles.summary: Critics worry that Polymarket's massive trading volumes and accurate predictions could undermine traditional democratic processes by turning elections into profit-driven spectacles. The platform's growing influence means regulators will likely scrutinize prediction markets more heavily, potentially changing how and where Americans can bet on politics.

articles.title: Iran war puts prediction markets back in the spotlight
articles.summary: Rising tensions with Iran are driving massive volume to geopolitical prediction markets as traders scramble to price military escalation odds. The spike proves these platforms shine brightest during crisis moments when traditional news can't capture the real-time probability shifts that actually matter to markets.

articles.title: Germany: Betting on elections is illegal — but possible
articles.summary: Germany bans election betting but enforcement gaps mean bettors can still place wagers through offshore platforms or creative workarounds. This regulatory gray area creates opportunities for savvy prediction market players while highlighting the challenge governments face trying to control cross-border betting flows.
